message:
test(frankenpandas): add prelude_completeness_compile_guard (br-6nexq / fd90.155)

fd90.155: the prelude expansion across fd90.121-fd90.144 added many
items but the existing prelude tests only exercised the OLD surface.
Items added by recent work — nan* primitives, MergeExecutionOptions,
MergeValidateMode, AsofDirection, DuplicateKeep, ConcatJoin, DropNaHow,
MergedDataFrame, GroupByError, TypeError, JoinError, etc. — weren't
exercised by any test.

A user accidentally removing one of these from the prelude wouldn't
surface a test failure unless the integration tests added in fd90.146
happened to depend on it.

Fix: add a 'prelude_completeness_compile_guard' unit test that
let-binds through every prelude item added by fd90.121-144. If anyone
removes one from the prelude, the test refuses to compile.

Coverage:
  - Join family: AsofDirection, JoinType, MergeValidateMode,
    MergeExecutionOptions, JoinError, MergedDataFrame
  - Errors: GroupByError, TypeError (top-level — imported via crate::)
  - Index enums: DuplicateKeep, ConcatJoin, DropNaHow
  - SQL: SqlIfExists, SqlConnection (trait)
  - All 23 nan* primitives — each called against a Vec<Scalar> sample
  - 5 concat variants — runtime-called subset
  - All 8 IO format readers + 9 of 10 writers (write_sql is generic;
    exercised separately in rusqlite_reexport_quickstart_compiles)
  - Module-level helpers: cut, qcut, timedelta_total_seconds,
    to_datetime_with_format/_options/_unit, merge_asof,
    merge_dataframes_on, merge_ordered, join_series

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib — 7 tests pass (was 6)
- cargo test -p frankenpandas — 7 lib + 2 integration + 2 doctests
  all pass

Pure additive test. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
